subject: UX & Research
title: The Calm Technology Movement
description: Learn The Calm Technology Movement in one minute.

hook.text: Mark Weiser at Xerox PARC envisioned 'calm technology' in 1995: tech that informs without demanding attention.

buildup.text: Today's devices scream for attention: notifications, badges, and alerts compete for your focus every second.

discovery.text: Calm tech uses the periphery: a light that changes color when it rains, an ambient display that glows with email volume.

twist.text: Amber Case revived calm technology principles in 2015, arguing that the best tech is the tech you don't notice.

climax.text: As tech surrounds us everywhere, calm design becomes essential. Attention is finite. Respect it or lose it.

punchline.text: The best technology fades into the background. Calm tech respects attention.

quiz.question: What is the core principle of calm technology?
options: Technology should inform without demanding attention, operating at the periphery of awareness
All notifications should be silenced permanently
Technology should only work when users are calm
